title: Define message envelope and causal context types
purpose: Defines the typed message envelope that wraps application payloads in the wire protocol, carrying schema identification and causal context alongside the payload bytes. Causal context is a first-class wire-level construct (P1) — the bus extracts ordering information from the frame without deserializing the application payload. Publish and ConversationMessage frames carry a MessageEnvelope, not raw bytes, ensuring that every message on the wire has type identity and causal ancestry.
task: Define the MessageEnvelope type containing schema_id (content type hash), causal_context, and payload bytes. Define the CausalContext type with an optional parent message reference and vector clock entry. Ensure MessageEnvelope serializes deterministically — same fields always produce same bytes. Wire Publish and ConversationMessage frames to carry MessageEnvelope instead of raw payload bytes. Ensure CausalContext is extractable from a message-carrying frame at the frame level without deserializing the application payload. Scope boundary: this brief defines the envelope and causal types and their serialization. It does not implement schema negotiation (PROTO-006), causal ordering enforcement (a bus-level concern), or payload validation (PROTO-006 + bus layer).

requirements.title: Define CausalContext with parent reference and vector clock entry
requirements.spec: CausalContext struct SHALL contain: parent_id (Option<MessageId> — a reference to the causally preceding message) and vector_clock_entry (Option<u64> — a logical timestamp for this message's position in the causal chain). CausalContext SHALL implement Debug, Clone, PartialEq, and Eq. An empty CausalContext (both fields None) represents a causally independent message.
acceptance: CausalContext has fields: parent_id (Option<MessageId>), vector_clock_entry (Option<u64>).
CausalContext::independent() returns a context with both fields None.
CausalContext::with_parent(id) returns a context with parent_id set.
CausalContext implements Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq.
MessageId is a newtype wrapping a byte array or String (not a raw primitive).

files: crates/liminal/src/protocol/causal.rs:1-1 — empty placeholder for the required protocol-level MessageId and CausalContext definitions.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/mod.rs:1-16 — causal module is already declared and protocol mod.rs follows the required pub-mod/pub-use-only convention.
crates/liminal/src/causal.rs:1-60 — existing core bus MessageId/CausalContext uses Uuid and a parent-only shape; useful style reference but does not satisfy protocol fields/vector clock.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:3-61 — small protocol type style: public fields, derives, #[must_use] constructors, to_wire/from_wire methods returning ProtocolError.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs:49-132 — use ProtocolError::codec for malformed bytes rather than panics or unwrap/expect.
context: protocol/causal.rs exists but is blank; the brief says create, but implementation should fill this file rather than add a second module.

There is already a root crate causal::MessageId wrapping Uuid with CausalContext { parent: Option<MessageId> }; do not import that as-is because R1 requires fields named parent_id and vector_clock_entry and a newtype wrapping bytes or String.
All public protocol types nearby derive Debug and usually Clone/Copy + Eq where possible; public constructors are marked #[must_use].
protocol/mod.rs already declares pub mod causal; if re-exporting new types, only add pub use lines there, no logic per CN3.
scout.approach: Implement protocol::causal::MessageId as a public newtype, preferably around String for a simple opaque stable wire representation or around a fixed byte array if you also define a wire length. Then define pub struct CausalContext { pub parent_id: Option<MessageId>, pub vector_clock_entry: Option<u64> } deriving Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq. Add const/regular constructors independent() -> both None and with_parent(id) -> parent set and vector_clock_entry None, plus Default delegating to independent if useful. Keep the root crate causal module untouched; qualify imports carefully as crate::protocol::causal when used by protocol envelope code.
scout.notes: Name collision is the main gotcha: crate::causal::CausalContext and crate::protocol::causal::CausalContext are distinct. If MessageId wraps String, later deterministic serialization must include a length prefix and UTF-8 validation/error handling; if it wraps [u8; 16] or [u8; 32], extraction becomes simpler but tests need deterministic sample bytes instead of generated IDs.

files_changed.note: Defined protocol MessageId newtype around String plus CausalContext with parent_id and vector_clock_entry fields, constructors, Default, trait derives, and unit coverage.
Re-exported CausalContext, MessageId, and extract_causal_context using pub use only.

dev.how: Added a distinct protocol::causal::MessageId newtype wrapping a stable UTF-8 String and a protocol::causal::CausalContext struct with Option<MessageId> parent_id and Option<u64> vector_clock_entry. CausalContext now supports independent() and with_parent(id), derives Debug/Clone/PartialEq/Eq, and remains separate from the existing core bus causal type.

checklist.note: CausalContext represents both optional parent message reference and optional vector clock entry.

stories.note: The causal context is a structured protocol type used by MessageEnvelope rather than application payload bytes.

requirements.title: Ensure CausalContext is extractable at frame level
requirements.spec: CausalContext SHALL be serialized as part of the MessageEnvelope in a position that allows extraction without deserializing the application payload. The serialization format SHALL place causal_context bytes before the payload bytes within the envelope, with a fixed-size or length-prefixed causal context section. This allows the bus to read causal information by parsing only the envelope header, not the application data.
acceptance: CausalContext is serialized before payload bytes in the envelope byte layout.
A function extract_causal_context(envelope_bytes) returns the CausalContext without parsing the payload portion.
extract_causal_context works on the raw envelope bytes from a Publish frame.
The causal context section has a known size or length prefix so the payload boundary is deterministic.

files: crates/liminal/src/protocol/causal.rs:1-1 — add causal-context wire encoding and extract_causal_context here per requirement file list.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/envelope.rs:1-1 — envelope serialization layout must place schema_id then causal_context_length then causal_context_bytes before payload_length/payload_bytes.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/payload.rs:50-129 — writer pattern for big-endian integers, length-prefixed bytes, checked offsets, and no panics.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/payload.rs:132-223 — reader pattern for deterministic parsing, trailing-byte rejection, and ProtocolError::codec on malformed data.
docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-002.json:33-48 — exact extraction acceptance: raw Publish frame envelope bytes, length/known-size causal section, no payload parsing.
context: The specified MessageEnvelope byte layout gives a natural extraction path: skip 32-byte schema_id, read big-endian u32 causal_context_length, parse only that slice, and ignore payload_length/payload_bytes.
PayloadReader/PayloadWriter are pub(super) under protocol::codec, so causal.rs/envelope.rs cannot directly use them unless visibility is widened or local equivalent parsing helpers are added.
Existing decode code treats malformed known-frame payloads as ProtocolError::CodecError and never panics; match that style with checked slice access and try_from errors.
R2 says extract_causal_context(envelope_bytes) in causal.rs, but the function logically parses MessageEnvelope bytes; avoid deserializing payload Vec or validating payload content.
scout.approach: Define a deterministic wire format for CausalContext itself in protocol/causal.rs, for example one-byte parent-present tag + optional length-prefixed MessageId bytes/string + one-byte vector-present tag + optional big-endian u64. Add serialize/deserialize helpers for CausalContext and a public extract_causal_context(envelope_bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<CausalContext, ProtocolError> that only reads the schema_id prefix and causal-context length from the envelope layout, then calls CausalContext deserialization on that slice. Use checked indexing and big-endian u32 parsing, and require exact consumption of causal_context_bytes but do not touch the payload bytes beyond locating the causal section boundary.
scout.notes: Do not make causal_context a separate Frame field: C18 requires it to serialize inside MessageEnvelope. Also ensure extract_causal_context works on the bytes that appear as the Publish frame's message payload after the channel field is decoded/located; if testing against a whole encoded Frame, account for the frame header and channel length prefix first or decode only far enough to obtain the envelope bytes.

files_changed.note: Added deterministic CausalContext wire encoding and extract_causal_context(envelope_bytes) that reads schema prefix and causal-context section only.
Envelope serialization places causal_context_length and causal_context bytes before payload_length and payload bytes.
Added coverage for extracting causal context from raw envelope bytes inside an encoded Publish frame.

dev.how: Implemented CausalContext serialization with presence tags, a u32 length-prefixed MessageId, and big-endian u64 vector-clock entries. MessageEnvelope serialization places schema_id, causal_context_length, causal_context_bytes, payload_length, and payload_bytes in that order; extract_causal_context parses only the schema prefix and length-delimited causal section and never deserializes payload bytes.

checklist.note: extract_causal_context returns CausalContext from serialized envelope bytes without parsing payload bytes, including bytes obtained from a Publish frame's envelope field.
Causal context is embedded inside MessageEnvelope serialization, not added as a separate frame field.

stories.note: Client authors can recover ordering metadata by parsing the envelope header/causal section only.

acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/envelope.rs:94-98` writes `schema_id`, then causal-context length/bytes, then payload length/bytes.

acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/causal.rs:220-235` reads only the 32-byte schema prefix, `causal_context_length`, and causal bytes, then deserializes those bytes; it never reads the payload section.

acceptance.criterion: extract_causal_context works on the raw envelope bytes from a Publish frame.

requirements.title: Define MessageEnvelope with schema ID, causal context, and payload
requirements.spec: MessageEnvelope struct SHALL contain: schema_id (SchemaId — a content type hash identifying the payload's schema), causal_context (CausalContext), and payload (Vec<u8> — opaque application data). SchemaId SHALL be a newtype wrapping a fixed-size byte array (e.g. [u8; 32] for SHA-256 hash). MessageEnvelope SHALL implement Debug and Clone.
acceptance: MessageEnvelope has fields: schema_id (SchemaId), causal_context (CausalContext), payload (Vec<u8>).
SchemaId is a newtype wrapping [u8; 32].
MessageEnvelope implements Debug and Clone.
MessageEnvelope::new(schema_id, causal_context, payload) constructs from all three fields.

files: crates/liminal/src/protocol/envelope.rs:1-1 — empty placeholder for SchemaId and MessageEnvelope.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/causal.rs:1-1 — MessageEnvelope must import/use protocol::causal::CausalContext, not the root bus type.
crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:8-35 — existing core SchemaId wraps Uuid; useful naming reference but R3 requires protocol SchemaId wrapping [u8; 32].
crates/liminal/src/envelope.rs:42-86 — existing core Envelope carries schema_id/causal_context/payload plus publisher/timestamp; do not modify for this protocol wire envelope.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/mod.rs:1-16 — envelope module already declared; optionally re-export MessageEnvelope/SchemaId here with pub use only.
context: protocol/envelope.rs is blank, while crate root envelope.rs already defines an internal bus Envelope with a different shape and extra metadata; keep the protocol wire type separate.
The existing channel::SchemaId wraps Uuid and is not suitable for R3 because the wire protocol requires a fixed 32-byte content-type hash.
Frame derives PartialEq/Eq, so for R5 it is useful for MessageEnvelope and SchemaId to derive PartialEq/Eq too, even though R3 only requires Debug and Clone.
Public type conventions include doc comments, #[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)] for data structs/newtypes, and #[must_use] on constructors/accessors.
scout.approach: In protocol/envelope.rs, define pub struct SchemaId(pub or private [u8; 32]) with a WIRE_LEN constant and constructors such as new(bytes: [u8; 32]) plus as_bytes()/into_bytes(). Define pub struct MessageEnvelope { pub schema_id: SchemaId, pub causal_context: CausalContext, pub payload: Vec<u8> } deriving Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, and add MessageEnvelope::new(schema_id, causal_context, payload). Use crate::protocol::causal::CausalContext or super::causal::CausalContext so the protocol envelope does not accidentally depend on the root bus Envelope/CausalContext.
scout.notes: Because an existing type named Envelope is re-exported at crate root, prefer the exact required name MessageEnvelope in protocol::envelope and consider pub use envelope::{MessageEnvelope, SchemaId} only under protocol, not crate root, to avoid confusing core bus APIs.

files_changed.note: Defined SchemaId([u8; 32]) and MessageEnvelope { schema_id, causal_context, payload } with constructor and trait derives.
Re-exported MessageEnvelope and SchemaId under protocol.

dev.how: Added a protocol-specific SchemaId newtype wrapping a fixed 32-byte hash and MessageEnvelope carrying schema_id, protocol CausalContext, and opaque Vec<u8> payload. MessageEnvelope::new constructs from all three fields and the public types implement Debug/Clone, with PartialEq/Eq also derived to support frame equality and round-trip tests.

checklist.note: MessageEnvelope contains schema_id, causal_context, and payload bytes with SchemaId as a [u8; 32] newtype.

requirements.title: Implement deterministic serialization for MessageEnvelope
requirements.spec: MessageEnvelope serialization SHALL be deterministic: the same schema_id, causal_context, and payload SHALL always produce the same byte sequence. The byte layout SHALL be: schema_id (32 bytes) + causal_context_length (u32) + causal_context_bytes + payload_length (u32) + payload_bytes. All multi-byte integers SHALL use big-endian byte order. Deserialization SHALL reconstruct the envelope losslessly.
acceptance: Two MessageEnvelopes with identical fields produce identical byte sequences.
Serialization followed by deserialization produces an equal MessageEnvelope.
The byte layout starts with the 32-byte schema_id.
Multi-byte integers use big-endian encoding.
An envelope with empty payload serializes and deserializes correctly.

files: crates/liminal/src/protocol/envelope.rs:1-1 — implement deterministic MessageEnvelope serialization/deserialization and tests here.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/causal.rs:1-1 — CausalContext serialization must be deterministic because its bytes are embedded in the envelope layout.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/payload.rs:21-35 — helper behavior to mirror for u32 length checks and overflow-safe length computation.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/payload.rs:60-94 — all existing multi-byte integers are encoded with to_be_bytes; match this for envelope lengths.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:22-60 — established API names serialize/to_wire_bytes/deserialize/from_wire_bytes and wrong-length error style.
context: Required layout is exact: 32-byte schema_id, 4-byte big-endian causal_context_length, causal_context_bytes, 4-byte big-endian payload_length, payload_bytes.

The current codec helpers are crate-private to protocol::codec; envelope.rs either needs its own small local checked read/write code or a refactor of helper visibility, but avoid creating cycles from protocol::codec into envelope.
encoded_len-style overflow checks matter: length fields are u32, so payload and causal bytes exceeding u32::MAX should return ProtocolError::CodecError, not truncate.
Acceptance asks deserialization reconstructs equal MessageEnvelope; deriving PartialEq/Eq for MessageEnvelope will make tests straightforward.
scout.approach: Add MessageEnvelope methods such as encoded_len(&self) -> Result<usize, ProtocolError>, serialize(&self) -> Result<Vec<u8>, ProtocolError>, write_to(&self, &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, ProtocolError> if you want zero-copy codec integration, and deserialize(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<Self, ProtocolError>. Serialize schema_id.as_bytes() first, serialize causal_context to a Vec<u8>, write causal length with u32::to_be_bytes, then causal bytes, then payload length and payload. Deserialization should use checked slice reads in the same order, reject truncated fields/trailing bytes via ProtocolError::codec, and support empty payload length 0.
scout.notes: The phrase 'infallible for valid frames' means do not introduce panics; it does not mean encode cannot return ProtocolError for impossible oversized buffers/lengths. Be careful that a naive serialize() allocating Vec can still fail before allocation only on u32 conversion/usize overflow; avoid unwrap on try_from in tests and production.

files_changed.note: Implemented encoded_len, serialize/to_wire_bytes, deserialize/from_wire_bytes, checked big-endian length parsing, and deterministic layout tests.
Provided deterministic CausalContext serialization used inside MessageEnvelope.

dev.how: MessageEnvelope now serializes deterministically as schema_id(32 bytes) + causal_context_length(u32 BE) + causal_context_bytes + payload_length(u32 BE) + payload_bytes. Deserialization uses checked slice reads, reconstructs equal envelopes including empty payloads, rejects malformed/trailing bytes via ProtocolError::codec, and never panics.

checklist.note: Identical envelopes produce identical byte sequences and deserialize losslessly with big-endian length fields.

stories.note: The deterministic layout keeps causal context before application payload bytes for simple SDK and bus parsing.

acceptance.evidence: `protocol::envelope::tests::identical_fields_produce_identical_bytes` (`crates/liminal/src/protocol/envelope.rs:261-267`) compares serialized bytes for two identical envelopes.

acceptance.criterion: Serialization followed by deserialization produces an equal MessageEnvelope.

acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/envelope.rs:94` writes `self.schema_id.as_bytes()` first; `protocol::envelope::tests::encoded_layout_starts_with_schema_id_and_big_endian_lengths` checks `encoded[..32]` at `envelope.rs:280-291`.

acceptance.evidence: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/envelope.rs:174-178` writes u32 lengths with `to_be_bytes`; `protocol::envelope::tests::encoded_layout_starts_with_schema_id_and_big_endian_lengths` checks causal and payload length bytes at `envelope.rs:292-302`.

acceptance.evidence: `protocol::envelope::tests::empty_payload_round_trips` (`crates/liminal/src/protocol/envelope.rs:306-314`) serializes, deserializes, and verifies an empty `Vec<u8>` payload.

requirements.title: Wire Publish and ConversationMessage frames to carry MessageEnvelope
requirements.spec: Frame::Publish and Frame::ConversationMessage variants SHALL carry a MessageEnvelope as their payload, not raw Vec<u8>. The codec's encode path for these frames SHALL serialize the MessageEnvelope into the frame payload. The codec's decode path SHALL deserialize the payload bytes into a MessageEnvelope. Other frame types SHALL NOT carry a MessageEnvelope.
acceptance: Frame::Publish has a field of type MessageEnvelope, not Vec<u8>.
Frame::ConversationMessage has a field of type MessageEnvelope, not Vec<u8>.
encode(Frame::Publish { envelope, .. }) serializes the envelope as the frame payload.
decode of a Publish frame returns Frame::Publish with a deserialized MessageEnvelope.
Frame::Ping, Frame::Subscribe, and other non-message frames do not contain MessageEnvelope.

files: crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:143-303 — Frame enum variants and new_publish constructor currently use raw Vec<u8>; change Publish and ConversationMessage to carry MessageEnvelope.

crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:135-182 — encoded_payload_len currently counts bytes_field_len(payload); update lengths to use serialized envelope bytes/length for Publish and ConversationMessage.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:212-301 — write_payload currently writes raw payload byte fields; update to write serialized MessageEnvelope bytes in the existing frame payload structure.

crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/known.rs:116-163 — decode Publish and ConversationMessage currently read bytes_field into Vec<u8>; change to MessageEnvelope::deserialize on the length-delimited bytes.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/tests.rs:23-72,266-301 — existing tests construct raw payload frames and assert raw payload preservation; update samples and add envelope round-trip assertions.
context: Publish frame payload currently consists of channel string field + bytes_field(payload); ConversationMessage consists of conversation_id + bytes_field(payload). R5 should keep the surrounding channel/conversation fields and make the length-delimited message bytes be serialized MessageEnvelope bytes.
Unknown frames are deliberately preserved as raw payload bytes in Frame::Unknown and should not carry MessageEnvelope.
Frame derives PartialEq/Eq; MessageEnvelope must implement PartialEq/Eq or the derive will break after adding envelope fields.

frame.rs is 471 lines and CN1 caps files at 500 lines; simply adding imports/tests can exceed the limit. The existing inline tests at lines 419-471 are the likely extraction target if this file grows.
scout.approach: Import MessageEnvelope into frame.rs and change Frame::Publish { payload: Vec<u8> } to { envelope: MessageEnvelope } and Frame::ConversationMessage { payload: Vec<u8> } likewise. Adjust new_publish to accept a MessageEnvelope (or a generic Into<MessageEnvelope> only if you define such conversion) and preserve existing stream validation. In codec.rs, compute envelope bytes length using MessageEnvelope::encoded_len or serialize once as appropriate, write them with writer.write_bytes_field(&envelope.serialize()?) after the channel/conversation_id fields, and in known.rs read the bytes field then call MessageEnvelope::deserialize. Update codec tests sample_frames and payload_bearing_frames_preserve_payload_bytes to build deterministic SchemaId/CausalContext/MessageEnvelope values and assert decoded envelopes equal originals; leave Ping/Subscribe/etc unchanged.
scout.notes: Watch for a possible double-allocation/duplicate serialization in encoded_payload_len and write_payload; acceptable for this brief unless you add a write_to method to MessageEnvelope. Also update any pattern matches in tests from payload to envelope. Because frame.rs is near 500 lines, move its #[cfg(test)] mod body into a new protocol/frame/tests.rs or trim comments if needed; do not put logic in mod.rs.

Updated encoded payload lengths and encode paths to length-prefix serialized MessageEnvelope bytes for message frames.
Updated decode paths to deserialize length-delimited envelope bytes into MessageEnvelope.
Updated frame samples and round-trip assertions for envelope-bearing Publish and ConversationMessage frames.
MessageEnvelope equality/serialization supports frame equality and codec round trips.

dev.how: Publish retains its channel field and ConversationMessage retains its conversation_id field, but their length-delimited message bytes are now serialized MessageEnvelope values. Decoding reconstructs MessageEnvelope for those two frame variants only; non-message frames and unknown frames remain unchanged and do not carry envelopes.

issues: CN1 hardening issue: `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs` was 480 lines, below the hard cap but close enough to the 500-line threshold that the brief says to extract into submodules when a module approaches the limit.
fixes: Extracted frame unit tests into `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame/tests.rs` and left only `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` at `crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:419-420`, reducing `frame.rs` to 420 lines while keeping `protocol::frame::tests::constructors_validate_streams` and `protocol::frame::tests::frame_type_discriminants_round_trip` passing.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement schema validation or schema negotiation — that is PROTO-006.
SHALL NOT implement causal ordering enforcement — that is a bus-level concern.
SHALL NOT define payload serialization format (protobuf, JSON) — payloads are opaque bytes.
SHALL NOT implement frame construction validation beyond what PROTO-001 establishes.
SHALL NOT add new frame types — use the types defined in PROTO-001.
verification: cargo check compiles envelope and causal modules with no errors.
Test: MessageEnvelope serialization is deterministic (two identical envelopes produce same bytes).
Test: CausalContext is extractable from raw envelope bytes without full deserialization.
Test: Publish and ConversationMessage frames round-trip with MessageEnvelope.
